## ❌ NEVER PROPOSE (hard rejections)

These proposal types are permanently rejected. Proposing them again wastes a swarm slot.

- API gateway / microservices — monolith works, bring data if proposing (Session 23)
- Redis — not needed until 2+ Railway instances (Session 23)
- ORM (SQLAlchemy, Prisma) — Supabase SDK only, non-negotiable
- Privacy by default (hidden scores) — CEO reviewed and chose public by default (Session 25)
- OpenAI as primary LLM — Gemini is primary, OpenAI is fallback, cost decision
- FastAPI WebSocket / ANUS real-time layer — deferred until 2026-07-01 or 500 active users (Session 25)
- Modal GPU self-hosted — Docker environment hell for solo founder (Session 48)
- ZEUS + fal.ai MuseTalk — not scalable, won 40/50 after correction (Session 48)
- D-ID video provider — not scalable, $5.90/mo = 20 vid/mo cap (Session 48)
- fal.ai/playai/tts wrong — model deprecated, switched to fal-ai/kokoro/american-english (Session 49)

## ✅ HIGH-VALUE PATTERNS (do more of these)

These types of proposals consistently get implemented or praised.

- Specific security vulnerabilities with CVSS score — always gets attention (Session 25)
- Storage/cost math — CTO missed the 14-43GB/year calculation, agent caught it (Session 25)
- User journey gaps — Leyla/Nigar persona reviews catch what code reviews miss (Session 25)
- Concrete file paths — 'fix line 39 in aura.py' > 'fix the auth issue' (Session 25)
- Calibrated estimates — '3 hours, not 3 days' with specific breakdown (Session 25)
- Route shadowing detection — Session 25 agent was RIGHT, CTO was WRONG (Session 42)
- Stored XSS via unescaped DeCE quotes from LLM — fixed with html.escape() (Session 42)
